<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The blog homepage uses a full-width three-column grid without a page header. Taxonomy pages currently show a header with centered pill-style term lists. Term pages are next.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The blog homepage format is now clear.

The homepage uses full-width three-column grids with dict-format cards and specific heading classes. Two templates need updating: taxonomy.html for all categories or tags, and term.html for individual terms.

Term pages will use full-width three-column grids with dict-format cards and dynamic headings. Pagination may be added. The taxonomy index page is trickier because it lists terms rather than blog posts.

The request most likely targets term pages. Taxonomy listing pages are less relevant.

Taxonomy pages will keep their term listings rather than showing blog cards. The homepage format omits the page-header entirely. It uses section--blog-list with a specific h1 typography inside the section.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The plan is clear. I will update the categories and tags pages to match the blog homepage format.

Individual category and tag pages will lose the gradient header. They will use a full-width three-column grid with homepage typography and dictionary-style blog cards. Pagination will be added for long lists. The taxonomy index page will get a cleaner grid of term cards.

I decided against extracting a reusable partial to keep the scope tight. I will update term.html and taxonomy.html directly.

The taxonomy index currently uses centered pill buttons. I will replace those with a grid of term cards that link to each category or tag.</details></think>
